msgs.text:
I read the prompt and the latest prior-log state. The prompt’s submission format fixes the distinguished tame generators sigma,tau, leaves tau^sigma=tau^2 implicit, requires later generators to lie in the pro-2 core, and uses exponents modulo M=85667662080.The only candidate still not refuted in the prior investigations is the dynamic tame-inertia relation variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1 equivalentlyr=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,π=π2,η=2π,with π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The latest prior frontier is no longer a raw finite quotient test. It is a normalized first-IA arithmetic-minus-candidate obstruction. For even f=2n, the mixed anti-cyclotomic part is modeled byAd=F2[Cd]≃F2 [T]/(Td−1),d=gcd(e,2n+1),plus two universal square coordinates. Turn 63 introduced exact-order projectors Ec(d)on Ad, and proposed using them to test whether the residual R(T) vanishes in each exact-order component.New contribution: the projector test is functorial only for the fiber-sum transfer

Turn 63’s projectors are correct. The missing structural point is how they interact with the anti-cyclotomic reductions d′ ∣d. This matters because the next tests are meant to distinguish a genuinely primitive d=9 residual from one already pulled back from d=3.

Let d′ ∣d, with m=d/d′ odd. Define the fiber-sum transferιd′,d:Ad′ ⟶Adbyιd′,d(Ui)=TiQd′(d)(T)=k=0∑m−1Ti+kd′,whereQd′(d)(T)=k=0∑m−1Tkd′.This is the correct algebraic shadow of “pulling up” an anti-cyclotomic residual along the odd fiber. It is not the naive coefficient embedding U↦Tm, and using the naive embedding would create false primitive components.

The map ιd′,d is an algebra isomorphism from Ad′ onto the idealQd′(d)Ad⊂Ad.

Indeed, Qd′(d)is an idempotent, and Td′Qd′(d)=Qd′(d), so multiplication is respected. On characters, Qd′(d)(ζ)=1 exactly when ord(ζ)∣d′, and 0 otherwise.

Now let Ec(d)be Turn 63’s exact-order projector:Qc(d)(T)=j=0∑d/c−1Tcj,Ec(d)(T)=r∣rad(c)∑Qc/r(d)(T).

Then the functoriality statement is:Ec(d)ιd′,d={ιd′,dEc(d′),0,c∣d′,c∤d′.Proof: evaluate at a d-th root ζ. The transfer ιd′,d vanishes on characters of order not dividing d′. On the remaining characters, exact order c is exactly the same exact-order condition seen inside Ad′. Since d is odd, Ad is semisimple over F2, so character evaluation proves the identity.

Consequence for the (9,6) test

For d=9 and d′=3,ι3,9 (1)=1+T3+T6,ι3,9 (U)=T+T4+T7,ι3,9(U2)=T2+T5+T8.

The primitive-9 projector isE9(9)=T3+T6.

The functoriality identity givesE9(9)ι3,9 (R)=0for every R∈A3.

So if the arithmetic-minus-candidate residual forL=Q2(ζ9,ϖ),ϖ9=2,has a nonzero E9(9)R, that is genuinely primitive-9 information. It cannot be explained by a residual already present at the d=3 anti-cyclotomic level.

Conversely, if E9(9)

R=0, then R lies in the transferred A3 -idealQ3(9)A9.

Concretely, in coefficient formR=i=0∑8riTi satisfies E9(9)

R=0 exactly whenr0=r3=r6,r1=r4=r7,r2=r5=r8.

After that, E3(9)

R=0 and E1(9)

R=0 are precisely the lower C3 -level tests. Thus the (9,6) test decomposes cleanly into:ϵb=0,ϵS=0,E9(9)

R=0,E3(9)

R=0,E1(9)R=0.

This is sharper than merely saying “apply E1,E3,E9.” It says E9 is the first genuinely primitive-9 obstruction, while E1,E3 are the transferred lower-level obstruction.

Critical status The candidate is still not proved. The prior computations establish a strong candidate-side package: correct finite-tame abelianizations, sign-kernel behavior, cup-product blocks, and a well-normalized first-IA obstruction space. The live relation has survived every recorded finite test. But the arithmetic residual has not been computed, and the dyadic formation/rigidity theorem is still missing. The remaining task is to show that the actual local Galois action lands at the same normalized affine origins as the candidate over every finite tame subextension, not merely that the candidate’s own residuals vanish.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I proved a new functoriality property for Turn 63’s exact-order projectors: under the correct fiber-sum transfer ιd′,d:Ad′→Ad, one hasEc(d)ιd′,d=ιd′,dEc(d′)for c∣d′, and zero otherwise. In particular, for d=9, E9(9)R is a genuinely primitive-9 obstruction and cannot come from a d=3 residual.

GAP: The arithmetic-minus-candidate residual (ϵb,ϵS;R(T)) has still not been computed for L=Q2(ζ9,ϖ), ϖ9=2. A dyadic formation theorem or explicit arithmetic comparison is still needed to prove the presentation.

NEXT: Compute the (9,6) arithmetic residual in the fixed τ,σ, normal-basis gauge. First test E9(9)

R; if it vanishes, descend the remaining residual through ι3,9−1 and apply the E3,E1 lower-level tests, together with ϵb=ϵS=0.[BREAKTHROUGH]
